questions.question: Why is the image formed by a plane mirror always virtual?
questions.answer: Because reflected rays diverge and only appear to meet behind the mirror.

questions.question: Why are convex mirrors used as rear-view mirrors in vehicles?
questions.answer: They provide a wider field of view and form erect, diminished images.

questions.question: Why can a concave mirror form both real and virtual images?
questions.answer: Because the image nature depends on the position of the object relative to the focus.

questions.question: What happens to the image when the object is placed at the focus of a concave mirror?
questions.answer: The reflected rays become parallel and the image forms at infinity.

questions.question: Why does a concave mirror produce a highly magnified image when the object is near the focus?
questions.answer: Because reflected rays diverge after reflection and appear to meet far from the mirror.

questions.question: What is the relationship between focal length and radius of curvature of a spherical mirror?
questions.answer: Focal length is half the radius of curvature (f = R/2).

questions.question: Why does light bend when it passes from one medium to another?
questions.answer: Because its speed changes in different media.

questions.question: What happens to the speed of light when it enters a denser medium?
questions.answer: The speed of light decreases.

questions.question: Why does light bend towards the normal in a denser medium?
questions.answer: Because the speed of light decreases in the denser medium.

questions.question: What happens when a ray of light passes along the normal during refraction?
questions.answer: It passes straight without bending.

questions.question: What does a refractive index greater than 1 indicate?
questions.answer: Light travels slower in that medium than in vacuum.

questions.question: Why does a coin appear raised when viewed through water?
questions.answer: Due to refraction of light at the water–air interface.

questions.question: What is the sign convention used in mirror formula calculations?
questions.answer: Distances are measured from the pole; right side positive and left side negative.

questions.question: Why are concave mirrors used in shaving mirrors?
questions.answer: They form an enlarged upright image when the object is between pole and focus.

questions.question: Why do stars appear to twinkle?
questions.answer: Due to refraction of light in different layers of Earth's atmosphere.

questions.question: What is magnification if the image height equals the object height?
questions.answer: Magnification is 1.

questions.question: What type of image is formed when magnification is negative?
questions.answer: A real and inverted image.

questions.question: What type of image is formed when magnification is positive?
questions.answer: A virtual and erect image.

questions.question: Why does a pencil partially immersed in water appear bent?
questions.answer: Due to refraction of light at the boundary between water and air.

questions.question: What happens to the refracted ray when the angle of incidence increases?
questions.answer: The angle of refraction also increases.

questions.question: Why does the bottom of a swimming pool appear shallower than it actually is?
questions.answer: Because light rays bend away from the normal when moving from water to air.

questions.question: What happens to the focal length of a mirror if its radius of curvature increases?
questions.answer: The focal length also increases.

questions.question: Why is the refractive index of glass greater than that of air?
questions.answer: Because light travels slower in glass than in air.

questions.question: What does the ratio sin i / sin r represent in refraction?
questions.answer: The refractive index of the medium.

questions.question: Why does a convex mirror always produce diminished images?
questions.answer: Because reflected rays diverge and appear to come from a point behind the mirror.
